mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicate robust demand outpacing supply constraints, allowing firms to raise prices and expand margins, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against a baseline model over a 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which directly boosts short-term equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >55% of observations during recovery phases, or the monthly Rank IC drops below 0.01 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns in >60% of months, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below random chance over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related consumer spending, which reduces near-term corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >65% of months where HOUST falls below 1200.0, or the threshold effect disappears when tested against a linear specification.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while consumer price inflation simultaneously accelerates, real borrowing costs increase and monetary policy tightens, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression over a 36-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms strong aggregate demand and healthy corporate profit environments, which sustains equity market momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% is followed by negative 60-day returns in >60% of quarters, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.02 during mid-cycle periods.
